name: ILI9xxx TFT LCD Series
description: With this display driver you can control the following displays:

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to re-draw the screen. Defaults to `60s`, use `never` to only manually update the screen via `component.update`.

config_entries.label: Model
config_entries.description: The model of the display. Options are:

config_entries.label: CS Pin
config_entries.description: The CS pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: DC Pin
config_entries.description: The DC pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Invert Colors
config_entries.description: Specifies whether the display colors should be inverted. Options are `true` or `false` - if you are unsure, use `false` and change if the colors are not as expected.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description:
**Set at most one of:** `pages`, `lambda`.

The lambda to use for rendering the content on the display. `it` will be an instance of <APIClass text="DisplayBuffer" path="display::DisplayBuffer" />. See [Display Rendering Engine](https://esphome.io/components/display#display-engine) for more information.

config_entries.label: Pages
config_entries.description:
**Set at most one of:** `pages`, `lambda`.

Show pages instead of a single lambda. See [Display Pages](https://esphome.io/components/display#display-pages).

config_entries.label: Reset Pin
config_entries.description: The RESET pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Auto Clear Enabled
config_entries.description: If the display should be cleared before each update. Defaults to `true` if a lambda or pages are configured, false otherwise.

config_entries.label: Color Order
config_entries.description: Should be one of `bgr` (default) or `rgb`.

config_entries.label: Color Palette
config_entries.description: When using 8 bit colors, this controls the type of color palette that will be used in the ESP's internal 8-bits-per-pixel buffer. This can be used to improve color depth quality of the image. For example if you know that the display will only be showing grayscale images, the clarity of the display can be improved by targeting the available colors to monochrome only. Options are:

config_entries.label: Color Palette Images
config_entries.description: A list of image files that will be used to generate the color palette for the display. This should only be used in conjunction with `color_palette: IMAGE_ADAPTIVE`. The images will be analysed at compile time and a custom color palette will be created based on the most commonly occuring colors. A typical setting would be a sample image that represented the fully populated display. This can significantly improve the quality of displayed images. Note that these images are not stored on the ESP device, just the 256byte color palette created from them.

config_entries.label: Dimensions
config_entries.description: Dimensions of the screen, specified either as *width* **x** *height* (e.g `320x240`  ) or with separate config keys. If not provided the dimensions will be determined by the model selected.

config_entries.label: Height
config_entries.description: Specifies height of display in pixels.

config_entries.label: Width
config_entries.description: Specifies width of display.

config_entries.label: Offset Height
config_entries.description: Specify an offset for the y-direction of the display. Default is 0.

config_entries.label: Offset Width
config_entries.description: Specify an offset for the x-direction of the display, typically used when an LCD is smaller than the maximum supported by the driver chip. Default is 0

config_entries.label: Init Sequence
config_entries.description: Allows custom initialisation sequences to be added. See below for more information.

config_entries.label: Pixel Mode
config_entries.description: Allows forcing the display into 18 or 16 bit mode. Options are `18bit` or `16bit`. If unspecified, the pixel mode will be determined by the model choice. Not all displays will work in both modes.

config_entries.label: Rotation
config_entries.description: Rotate the display presentation in software. Choose one of `0°`, `90°`, `180°`, or `270°`. This option cannot be used with `transform`.

config_entries.label: Transform
config_entries.description: Transform the display presentation using hardware. All defaults are `false`. This option cannot be used with `rotation`.

config_entries.label: Mirror X
config_entries.description: If true, mirror the x axis.

config_entries.label: Mirror Y
config_entries.description: If true, mirror the y axis.

config_entries.label: Swap Xy
config_entries.description: If true, exchange the x and y axes.
